get.summary: Gets all actions.
get.description:
This endpoint supports OData filtering.
You can use the following request to retrieve actions that match the specified filters.
Example: api/workflow/v1/actions?$filter=name like 'example' The following action properties are available for OData, and they have specific support for the $filter option.
id - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le, in, nin name - $filter eq, ne, like sortorder - $filter eq, ne, like script - $filter like systemId - $filter eq, ne, like actionType - $filter eq, ne placement - $filter eq, ne, like actionDisplayLevel - $filter eq, ne image - $filter eq, ne, like globalCondition - $filter eq, ne, like preActionCondition - $filter eq, ne, like postActionCondition - $filter eq, ne, like modifiedBy - $filter eq, ne, like modifiedon - $filter eq, lt, gt, ge, le Additional OData options are available: $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} {default is name} $top={number} {default is 10, max is 1000} $skip={number} This endpoint response also can also be expanded with additional data: $expand=translations This endpoint has specific support for the $search option:
$search={some_value} You can use search option by fields: systemId, name, sortOrder If you need to search the action name for a specific language, you can specify the dictionary identifier.
Example: $search={some_value}&dictionaryId={dictionary_identifier} Property description: actionType - Two types of action are available. 0 - Standard 1 - Custom use above vaule for filtering.

post.description:
This endpoint creates a new custom action with the provided action payload.

Properties description:
            
    sortorder - The default value is "1000000". If this property has a blank value or isn't provided in the payload, then the default sortorder is used.
    placement - The following values are valid: 
                    | Values | Description               |
                    |________|___________________________|
                    | C      | Quote page                |
                    | L      | Load Quote page           |
                    | LC     | Quote and Load quote page |
                    |________|___________________________|                  
    
    If Placement property value is “L“ then following propeties are not considered:
        sortOrder
        actionDisplayLevel
        image

parameters.name: changeSetId
parameters.description: Change set identifier (if omitted, the default change set is used).

get.summary: Gets action translation data using the action identifier and the dictionary identifier.
get.description: This endpoint retrieves the translation for an action using its action identifier and the dictionary identifier.

200.description: Translation for the specified action and dictionary.

404.description: Action or translation are not found.

get.summary: Gets the action using its identifier.
get.description: This endpoint retrieves an action using its identifier.

put.description:
This endpoint updates the action with the specified action identifier with the provided action payload.

Properties description:

    sortorder - The default value is "1000000". If this property has a blank value or isn't provided in the payload, then the default sortorder is used.
    placement - The following values are valid: 
                    | Values | Description               |
                    |________|___________________________|
                    | C      | Quote page                |
                    | L      | Load Quote page           |
                    | LC     | Quote and Load quote page |
                    |________|___________________________|
                
    systemId  - Consider for Custom action only.
    script    - Consider for Custom action only.
    placement - Consider for Custom action only.
    forceProxyGeneration - Consider for Custom action only.     
    
    If Custom action's Placement property value is “L“ then following propeties are not considered:
        sortOrder
        actionDisplayLevel
        image

parameters.description: Change set identifier (if omitted, the default change set is used).

Properties description:

    placement - The following values are valid: 
                    | Values | Description               |
                    |________|___________________________|
                    | C      | Quote page                |
                    | L      | Load Quote page           |
                    | LC     | Quote and Load quote page |
                    |________|___________________________|
                
    systemId  - Consider for Custom action only.
    script    - Consider for Custom action only.
    placement - Consider for Custom action only.
    forceProxyGeneration - Consider for Custom action only. 
    
    If Custom action's Placement property value is “L“ then following propeties are not considered:
        sortOrder
        actionDisplayLevel
        image

get.summary: Get custom action script version history list for the given action identifier.
get.description:
This endpoint will get custom action script version history list for the given action identifier. This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Example: api/workflow/v1/actions/1/versions?$filter=changedBy like 'smith' The following custom action version properties are available for OData, and have specific support for the $filter option:
content - $filter eq,ne,like changedBy - $filter eq,ne,like created - $filter eq,lt,gt,ge,le Additional OData options are available for the properties listed above:
$orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number} {default is 10, max is 100} $skip={number} SAP CPQ custom actions script API is using the oData convention.

200.description: The paged list contains all custom action versions.

get.summary: Gets a unique System ID based on the provided action name.
get.description:
You can use the following request to get a unique System ID for the provided name. The name is created by adding suffix cpq to the provided name. ExampleName -> ExampleName_cpq
If the name is not unique, an integer is added to the suffix. ExampleName -> ExampleName_1_cpq
This endpoint uses query parameter ?name
Example: api/workflow/v1/actions/systemId?name=ExampleName

get.summary: Gets the specified approval rule by its Identifier.

Use this endpoint to retrieve approval rule by its identifier.

Note:

Possible ApprovalRequirementsChoice values:  

      0 - No specific requirements selected.
      1 - User can choose from the list of approvers.
      2 - User can choose only one approver.
      
Possible ConditionApproverType values:

      0 - User. 1 - Hierarchy. 2 - DynamicApprover.
      
Possible HierarchyType values:

      0 - Ordering parent. 1 - Managing parent. 2 - Approving parent.

get.summary: Gets all approval rules.
get.description:
This endpoint supports OData filtering.
You can use the following request to retrieve approval rules that match the specified filters.
Example: api/workflow/v1/approvalRules?$filter=conditionname like 'example' The following action properties are available for OData, and they have specific support for the $filter option.
id - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le, in, nin name - $filter eq, ne, like conditionname - $filter eq, ne, like description - $filter eq, ne, like approveconditiondescription - $filter eq, ne, like userid - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le, in, nin username - $filter eq, ne, like usertypeid - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le, in, nin usertypename - $filter eq, ne, like companyid - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le, in, nin companyname - $filter eq, ne, like condition - $filter eq, ne, like recalculatedcondition - $filter eq, ne, like Additional OData options are available: $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} {default is name} $top={number} {default is 10, max is 1000} $skip={number} This endpoint response also can also be expanded with additional data: $expand=translations This endpoint has specific support for the $search option:
$search={some_value} You can use search option by fields: conditionname, conditiondescription, username, companyname, usertypename If you need to search the approval rule conditionname or conditiondescription for a specific language, you can specify the dictionary identifier.
Example: $search={some_value}&dictionaryId={dictionary_identifier} Note: Possible ApprovalRequirementsChoice values: 0 - No specific requirements selected. 1 - User can choose from the list of approvers. 2 - User can choose only one approver. Possible ConditionApproverType values: 0 - User. 1 - Hierarchy. 2 - DynamicApprover. Possible HierarchyType values: 0 - Ordering parent. 1 - Managing parent. 2 - Approving parent.

post.description:
You can use this endpoint to create new approval rule.

Possible ApprovalRequirementsChoice values:  
      0 - No specific requirements selected.
      1 - User can choose from the list of approvers.
      2 - User can choose only one approver.
      
Possible ConditionApproverType values:  
      0 - User.
      1 - Hierarchy.
      2 - DynamicApprover.
      
Possible HierarchyType values:  
      0 - Ordering parent.
      1 - Managing parent.
      2 - Approving parent.

get.summary: Gets attributes
get.description:
This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Example: /api/products/v1/attributes?$filter=name like 'memory' The following attribute properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $filter option:
name - $filter eq,ne,like systemId - $filter eq,ne,like type - $filter eq,ne,like typeLabel - $filter eq,ne,like, numberOfValues - $filter eq,lt,gt,ge,le externalId - $filter eq,ne,like modifiedBy - $filter eq,ne,like modifiedOn - $filter eq,lt,gt,ge,le containsPersonallyIdentifiableInformation - $filter eq,ne isSyncedFromBackOffice - $filter eq,ne isDynamic - $filter eq,ne The following attribute properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $search option:
Example: $search={some_value} Properties: name Additional OData options are available for properties listed above:
$orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number} {default is 10, max is 100} $skip={number} Additional query strings:
dictionaryId - translates attribute fields for the provided dictionary. If translation for a certain attribute field doesn't exist, it will return the value from the definition. This query string needs to be an integer. For more information look at OData syntax documentation: https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/

post.description:
AttributeVM needs to follow attribute validation rules.

The following attribute types are available:
    1 - User Selection w/ Quantity Inputs for each Attribute Value
    2 - Autocomplete - Sourced from a Custom Table
    3 - Autocomplete - Sourced from a Quote Table
    4 - Container
    5 - User Input or Computed - Date
    6 - External Value
    7 - Measurement
    8 - User Selection
    9 - User Input or Computed - Number
    10 - User Selection w/ Attribute Quantity Input
    11 - User Input or Computed - String
    12 - Contract Duration
    13 - Billing Cycle

201.description: Attribute is created. The route to the attribute can be found in response header's 'location' section.

get.summary: Get all categories.

You can use the following request to retrieve categories that match the specified filters. This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Example: /api/products/v1/categories?$filter=name eq 'example' /api/products/v1/categories?$filter=systemId ne 'example' /api/products/v1/categories?$filter=active eq 'true' /api/products/v1/categories?$filter=parentCategory eq '1' The following attribute properties are available to OData, and they have a specific support for the $filter option.
id - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le name - $filter eq, ne, like systemId - $filter eq, ne, like description - $filter eq, ne, like active - $filter eq, ne, like firmsOnly - $filter eq, ne, like rank - $filter eq, ne, like mainImage - $filter eq, ne, like additionalImage1 - $filter eq, ne, like additionalImage2 - $filter eq, ne, like mobileImage - $filter eq, ne, like imageAltText - $filter eq, ne, like pluginRedirect - $filter eq, ne, like startDate - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le endDate - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le defaultCatalogueViewId - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le propagateCatalogueView - $filter eq, ne, like visibleToEveryone - $filter eq, ne, like parentCategory - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le The following attribute properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $search option:
Example: $search={some_value} Properties: name Additional OData options are available for these properties: $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc}
$top={number, max 100}
$skip={number}
$expand=permissions

This endpoint uses query parameter ?name
Example: /api/products/v1/categories/systemId?name=ExampleName

get.summary: Gets the configuration by the specified configuration ID.
get.description:
The following query parameter is supported:
$expand: use this parameter to specify the list of sub nodes which need to be included in the response. By default, only the configuration is returned without selected attributes.
The keywords for $expand are: All, SelectedAttributes and ConfigurationContext.
Example 1: $expand = SelectedAttributes (selected attributes will be added in response, inside configuration node). Example 2: $expand = ConfigurationContext (configurationContext will be added in response, besides the configuration).

get.summary: Gets the configuration.
get.description:
This endpoint supports the OData $select filter.
If the $select filter is omitted, the entire configuration will be returned. When the $select filter is specified, only the configuration ID, information about whether the configuration is complete, and the product details of the root product will be displayed, along with the data specified in the $select filter. You can include the following information using the $select filter: - pricingDetails: Includes product, attribute, and attribute value pricing details. - tabs: Includes product tabs. - attributes: Includes product attributes. - values: Includes product attribute values.Values will be visible only if the attributes filter is active. - description: Includes product description, attribute description, hint, and attribute value description. - messages: Includes configuration messages. - containers: Includes containers. In the case of a parent/child product, all selected filters will be propagated to the child product. Example: /api/configuration/v2/configurations?$select=attributes,containers /api/configuration/v2/configurations?$select=containers For more information, please refer to the OData syntax documentation: https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/

Note: This controller allows setting the 'Accept-Language' header to serve content in the client's preferred language.
Examples:
To request content in French, the client can send a request with the following header:
Accept-Language: fr-FR This will prompt the application to respond in French if translation is configured.
Multiple languages can also be specified, and the server will attempt to serve content in the highest priority language available:
Accept-Language: es-ES, fr-FR;q=0.8, en-US;q=0.5 In this example, Spanish (`es-ES`) has the highest priority, followed by French (`fr-FR`) with a lower quality value (`q=0.8`), and English (`en-US`) with the lowest priority (`q=0.5`).
For more information, please refer to the Mozilla documentation: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Accept-Language

get.summary: Refreshes the session.

post.summary:
Logs the user in with JWT.
To use this method you must first define shared secret in Setup/Application parameters.
Algorithm used is HS256 so header is
header:
 {
    "typ": "JWT",
    "alg": "HS256"
 }
            
What we ecpect in JWT payload:
 - exp (Expiration which didn't expire yet)
 - username - existing CPQ User's username
 - domain - CPQ tenant name
            
payload:
 {
    "exp": 1488893055,
    "username": "my_username",
    "domain": "my_tenant_name"
 }
            
Token should be sent in request authorization header like below.
Authorization: Bearer [token]
Generates **JWT** token using **OWIN** token for Authentication
post.description:
Token will be look like this when decoded: { "exp": 1593595496, "username": "USERNAME", "domain": "DOMAIN" } Token is encoded with **HS256** and signature is verified using **JWTSharedSecret**.
Domain and username are taken from Owin token if Authentication is valid.

Example: $search={some_value} If you need to search the country name for a specific language, you can specify the dictionary identifier.

Example: api/quote/v1/documentTemplates?$filter=name like 'my template' The following document template properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $filter option:
systemId - $filter eq,ne,like name - $filter eq,ne,like filename - $filter eq,ne,like description - $filter eq,ne,like createdBy - $filter eq,ne,like modifiedBy - $filter eq,ne,like datemodified - $filter eq,ne templateType - $filter eq,ne rank - $filter eq,ne sectionsinfo.sections.name - $filter eq,ne,like visibiltyPermissionsInfo.permissions.name - $filter eq,ne,like parenttemplates.name - $filter eq,ne,like The following template properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $search option:
Example: $search="my template" Properties: name Additional OData options are available for properties listed above:
Example: api/quote/v1/documentTemplates?$skip=0&$top=20&$orderby=name asc&$expand=revisions $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number} {default is 10, max is 100} $skip={number} $expand=all $expand=revisions $expand=relatedFields $expand=sectionsInfo $expand=visibilityPermissionsInfo $expand=formatPermissionsInfo $expand=additionalFilesInfo $expand=attachmentsInfo $expand=delegationOfAuthorityInfo $expand=parentDocumentTemplates Additional query strings:
dictionaryId - translates document template name and description for the provided dictionary. If translation for a certain template doesn't exist, it will return the value from the definition. This query string needs to be an integer. Example: api/quote/v1/documentTemplates?$skip=0&$top=20&$orderby=name asc&$expand=revisions&dictionaryId=1 SAP CPQ document templates API is using oData convention.

The following Email Settings Types are available:
    1 - Users
    2 - DocumentGeneration
    3 - AdminNotifications
    4 - WorkflowNotifications
    5 - ApprovalAutoReminder
    6 - QuoteExpiration
    7 - DocuSign (only for Quote 1.0 engine)

The following Smtp Types are available:
    1 - SapSmtpService
    2 - CustomSmtpService
    3 - CpqLegacyService

The following Smtp Authentication Types are available:
    1 - None
    2 - UsernameAndPassword
    3 - OAuth

The following OAuth Provider Types are available:
    1 - Microsoft
            
The following From Email Address Source Types are available:
    1 - None (it is applicable only for 'SapSmtpService' Smtp Type)
    2 - CurrentUserEmail
    3 - CustomEmail (if selected 'FromEmailAddress' value is required)
            
The following From Display Name Source Types are available:
    1 - None
    2 - CurrentUserFullName
    3 - CustomName (if selected 'FromDisplayName' value is required)
            
The following Sender Email Address Source Types are available:
    1 - None
    2 - CurrentUserEmail
    3 - CustomEmail (if selected 'SenderEmailAddress' value is required)
            
The following Sender Display Name Source Types are available:
    1 - None
    2 - CurrentUserFullName
    3 - CustomName (if selected 'SenderDisplayName' value is required)

The following Replay-To Email Address Source Types are available:
    1 - None
    2 - CurrentUserEmail
    3 - CustomEmail (if selected 'ReplayToEmailAddress' value is required)
            
The following ReplayTo Display Name Source Types are available:
    1 - None
    2 - CurrentUserFullName
    3 - CustomName (if selected 'ReplayToDisplayName' value is required)

404.description: Email settings with the requested id can not found.

409.description: The id of Email settings doesn't match the id from the route.

get.summary: Gets the Event logs.
get.description:
You can use the following request to get Event logs. This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Example: /api/EventLog/v1/EventLogs?$filter=title eq 'example' /api/EventLog/v1/EventLogs?$filter=sessionid ne 'example' /api/EventLog/v1/EventLogs?$filter=userid eq '1' The following attribute properties are available to OData, and they have a specific support for the $filter option.
id - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le id_quickfilter - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le id_seekpagination - $filter eq, ne, like loggedat - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le loggedat_quickfilter - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le level - $filter eq, ne, like labels - $filter eq, ne, like details - $filter eq, ne, like title - $filter eq, ne, like description - $filter eq, ne, like domain - $filter eq, ne, like logger - $filter eq, ne, like userid - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le username - $filter eq, ne, like sessionid - $filter eq, ne, like cartid - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le cartcompositenumber - $filter eq, ne, like applicationpoolip - $filter eq, ne, like exception - $filter eq, ne, like performeripaddress - $filter eq, ne, like The following attribute properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $search option:
Example: $search={some_value} Properties: title, labels Additional OData options are available for these properties: $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc}
$top={number, max 100}
$skip={number}

get.summary: Gets all global scripts.
get.description:
This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Example: /api/script/v1/globalscripts?$filter=name like 'script1' /api/script/v1/globalscripts?$select=modifiedBy,modifiedOn,id,name,executionevent The following global script properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $filter option:
name - $filter eq,ne,like active - $filter eq,ne modifiedBy - $filter eq,ne,like modifiedOn - $filter eq,lt,gt,ge,le script - $filter eq,ne,like systemId - $filter eq,ne,like description - $filter eq,ne,like isModule - $filter eq,ne isCSRFProtected - $filter eq,ne executionevent - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin Additional OData options are available for properties listed above:
$orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number} {default is 10, max is 100} $skip={number} $select={properties name} Note: In case of Select events using executionevent property, id will be included in response as it was required to fetch events. This endpoint have specific support for $search option:
$search={some_value} You can use search option by fields: name SAP CPQ global script API is using oData convention.

$skip={number}
This endpoint response also can be expanded with additional data: $expand=translations (for translations connected to item actions)

kdId - $filter eq, ne, like firstFailure - $filter eq, ne, like, gt, lt lastFailure - $filter eq, ne, like, gt, lt errorMessage - eq, ne, like Furthermore, an additional OData option is available for the properties above:

get.summary: Get all Likelihood of Approvals.

200.description: List of all Likelihood of Approvals.

post.summary: Creates a new likelihood of approval.
post.description:
You can use the following request to create a new likelihood of approval.
Properties description: productTypeId - The product type identifier. name - The name of the likelihood of approval. type - The Type of Approval probability field, the only following values are valid: ProductType, Total field - The Approval probability field, the only following values are valid: If type equals “ProductType“ If type equals “Total“ _____________________________________ _______________________________________ | Id |Value | | Id |Value | | ______|____________________________| | ______|_____________________________ | |181 |PTDiscountPercent | |243 |SubTotalAmount | |182 |PTDiscountAmount | |244 |TotalChannelMarkupPercent | |179 |PTMultiplier | |245 |TotalChannelMarkupAmount | |180 |PTMultiplierAmount | |248 |TotalGrossMarginPercent | |183 |PTSubTotal | |249 |TotalGrossMarginAmount | |184 |PTChannelMarkupPercent | |234 |TotalNetPrice | |185 |PTChannelMarkupAmount | |235 |AverageProductDiscountPercent | |246 |EndUserSubTotalAmount | |236 |TotalProductDiscountAmount | |192 |PTGrossMarginPercent | |237 |AverageProductMultiplier | |193 |PTGrossMarginAmount | |238 |TotalProductMultiplierAmount | |195 |PTChannelGrossMarginPercent | |251 |TotalChannelGrossMarginPercent| |196 |PTChannelGrossMarginAmount | |252 |TotalChannelGrossMarginAmount | |_______|____________________________| |_______|______________________________| If the value of the Type property is equal to “Total“ then following propeties are not considered: productTypeId

parameters.name: likelihoodOfApproval
parameters.description: The likelihood of approval to create.

201.description: The specified likelihood of approval was created successfully.

get.summary: Get the likelihood of approval by its identifier.
get.description: Use this endpoint to retrieve likelihood of approval by its identifier.

200.description: The likelihood of approval with the specified identifier.

404.description: The specified likelihood of approval not found.

put.summary: Updates the specified likelihood of approval.
put.description:
You can use this endpoint to update an likelihood of approval.
   Properties description:

        productTypeId - The product type identifier.
        name - The name of the likelihood of approval.
        type - The Type of Approval probability field, the only following values are valid: ProductType, Total
        field - The Approval probability field, the only following values are valid:
        
          If type equals “ProductType“                  If type equals “Total“     
          _____________________________________       _______________________________________
          | Id    |Value                       |      | Id    |Value                         |
          | ______|____________________________|      | ______|_____________________________ | 
          |181    |PTDiscountPercent           |      |243    |SubTotalAmount                |                         
          |182    |PTDiscountAmount            |      |244    |TotalChannelMarkupPercent     |
          |179    |PTMultiplier                |      |245    |TotalChannelMarkupAmount      | 
          |180    |PTMultiplierAmount          |      |248    |TotalGrossMarginPercent       |
          |183    |PTSubTotal                  |      |249    |TotalGrossMarginAmount        |
          |184    |PTChannelMarkupPercent      |      |234    |TotalNetPrice                 |
          |185    |PTChannelMarkupAmount       |      |235    |AverageProductDiscountPercent |
          |246    |EndUserSubTotalAmount       |      |236    |TotalProductDiscountAmount    |
          |192    |PTGrossMarginPercent        |      |237    |AverageProductMultiplier      |
          |193    |PTGrossMarginAmount         |      |238    |TotalProductMultiplierAmount  | 
          |195    |PTChannelGrossMarginPercent |      |251    |TotalChannelGrossMarginPercent|
          |196    |PTChannelGrossMarginAmount  |      |252    |TotalChannelGrossMarginAmount |
          |_______|____________________________|      |_______|______________________________|
            

        If the value of the Type property is equal to “Total“ then following propeties are not considered:
        productTypeId

200.description: The specified likelihood of approval was updated successfully.

404.description: The likelihood of approval with the provided identifier was not found.

409.description: The identifier of likelihood of approval model doesn't match the identifier from the route.

delete.summary: Deletes the specified Likelihood of Approval.
delete.description: You can use the following endpoint to delete Likelihood of Approval with provided identifier.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The target Likelihood of Approval identifier.

204.description: The specified Likelihood of Approval was deleted successfully.

404.description: The specified Likelihood of Approval not found.

get.description:
The following attribute types are avaliable:
    1 - User Selection w/ Quantity Inputs for each Attribute Value
    2 - Autocomplete - Sourced from a Custom Table
    3 - Autocomplete - Sourced from a Quote Table
    4 - Container
    5 - User Input or Computed - Date
    6 - External Value
    7 - Measurement
    8 - User Selection

    11 - User Input or Computed - String
    12 - Contract Duration
    13 - Billing Cycle
    
The following call example will return only User Selection attributes:
    setup/api/v1/admin/lookups/attributes/type/8

The following call example will return all Autocomplete attributes:
    setup/api/v1/admin/lookups/attributes/type/2,3
    
Note: This endpoint can retrun attributes of different types, you just need to specifie attribute types as comma separated values following the above list.

id - $filter eq, ne subject - $filter eq, ne, like type - $filter eq, ne (available types: Plain text - 0, Html - 1) Additional OData options are available for these properties: $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number, max 1000} $skip={number} This endpoint response also can be expanded with additional data: $expand=attachments (for notification attachments details) $expand=emaillists (for notification email lists details)

get.summary: Get all statuses.
get.description:
This endpoint supports OData filtering.
You can use the following request to retrieve order statuses that match the specified filters.
Example: /api/workflow/v1/orderStatuses?$filter=name like 'example' The following attribute properties are available to OData, and they have a specific support for the $filter option.
id - $filter eq, ne, like, lt, gt, ge, le, in, nin name - $filter eq, ne, like rank - $filter eq, ne, like, gt, lt, ge, le, in, nin isDefault - $filter eq, ne isLocked - $filter eq, ne Additional OData options are available for these properties: $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number, max 1000} $skip={number} This endpoint response also can be expanded with additional data: $expand=translations You can use specific $search option for searching status by name:
Example: $search={some_value} If you need to search the status name for a specific language, you can specify the dictionary identifier.
Example: $search={some_value}&dictionaryId={dictionary_identifier}

You can use this endpoint to create new Status with rank.

IsLocked - Locks the quote(readonly) if this flag is true.

get.description:
This endpoint supports filtering by the following properties:
name, description, members, permissionType
            
permissionType values:

     0 - Custom permission groups
     1 - User type
     2 - Company
     3 - Market (when EnableSalesArea is set to False)
     4 - Brand
     5 - User
     6 - Sales organization (when EnableSalesArea is set to True)
            
Example:
            
    /setup/api/v1/admin/permissionGroups?permissionType_eq=1

If permissionType filter is not used custom permission groups are returned.

post.description:
This is the bulk insert method for pricing procedures. If back-end system is connected, then determinations for pricing procedure are synchronized automatically. If the request is not valid or a condition is not satisfied, all pricing procedures are rejected and an error response is displayed.
Conditions for the insert method are the following:
    
        1. There is no ID, or the ID for the pricing procedure is 0.
        2. The pricing procedure name is unique.

get.description:
This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Example: /api/pricingProcedure/v1/procedures/{pricingProcedureId}/determinations?$filter=salesOrganization eq 'example' /api/pricingProcedure/v1/procedures/{pricingProcedureId}/determinations?$filter=salesOrganization ne 'example' /api/pricingProcedure/v1/procedures/{pricingProcedureId}/determinations?$filter=documentPricingProcedure like 'example' /api/pricingProcedure/v1/procedures/{pricingProcedureId}/determinations?$filter=salesOrganization eq '0001' and distributionChannel ne '01' and division like '01' The following pricing procedure properties are available via OData, and specifically support the $filter option.
salesOrganization - $filter eq,ne,like distributionChannel - $filter eq,ne,like division - $filter eq,ne,like, documentPricingProcedure - $filter eq,ne,like, customerPricingProcedure - $filter eq,ne,like The following attribute properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $search option:
Example: $search={some_value} Properties: salesOrganization Furthermore, an additional OData option is available for the properties above:
$orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number, max 100} $skip={number} For more information, please refer to the OData syntax documentation: https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/

get.summary: Gets Products DEPRECATED
get.description:
Gets filtered, paged and sorted products with total number of products
Example: setup/api/v1/admin/products?name_like=memory setup/api/v1/admin/products?productTypeByName_like=test&sort=-partNumber&offset=0&limit=10&dictionaryId=1 The following product properties are available and can be used for filtering:
name - eq,neq,ne,like partNumber - eq,neq,ne,like orderItemType - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin type - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin - it represents ID of the type productTypeByName - like category - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin - it represents ID of the category categoryByName - like permissions - like displayType - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin versionNumberExact - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin modifiedDate - neq,ne,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le modifiedBy - eq,neq,ne,like active - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin isSyncedFromBackOffice - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin isSimple - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin description - like systemId - eq,neq,ne,like isSyncedFromBackOffice - eq,neq,ne,like isSapProduct - eq,neq,ne,like orderItemType - eq,neq,ne,like configuraitonType - eq,neq,ne,like aliasOf - eq,neq,ne,like product_id - eq,neq,ne,like,lt,gt,geq,ge,leq,le,in,nin systemId - eq,neq,ne,like product_name - eq,neq,ne,like Quick Filter (quickFilter) is performed based on name or partNumber:
Example: quickFilter={some_value} Additional options are available
sort={- in case of descending order}{properties name} limit={number} {default is 10, max is 100} - number of records per page offset={number} - page number Total number of pages can be calculated as 'total number of products' / limit Additional query strings:
dictionaryId - translates product fields for the provided dictionary. If translation for a certain product field doesn't exist, it will return the value from the definition. This query string needs to be an integer.

get.description:

get.summary: Retrieves a list of products that match the specified query conditions.
get.description:
### Performance Considerations:
- Retrieving **large datasets** (e.g., **1000+ products**) with `$expand=all` **may cause performance issues** due to the heavy response.
- If the system uses **many dictionaries**, consider **omitting translations** to improve performance.
- **BasicInfo** is **always included** in the response.
            
### Supported Query Parameters:
- **`$expand`** (Include additional details in response):
  - By default, only basic product information is returned.
  - Supported values:
    - `All` → Returns **all details**.
    - `Attributes`, `GlobalScripts`, `LocalScripts`, `AliasAttributesInfo`, `AdvancedRules`,
      `SimpleRules`, `ProductValidationMessages`, `Layout`, `CodeMatching`, `Translations`, `SalesUnitsOfMeasure`, `VisibilityRestrictions`
  - **Example:**  
    - `$expand=All` → Returns all details.  
    - `$expand=Attributes` → Returns **only Attributes** + BasicInfo.
            
- **`$top` and `$skip`** (Pagination):
  - `$top`: Number of items to return (default **10**, max **1000**).
  - `$skip`: Number of items to **skip** before returning results.
            
- **`$filter`** (Filter products based on conditions):
  - Conditions are combined using `and`. (e.g., /api/product/v1/Products?$filter=Name eq 'Product1' and SystemId eq '123'')
  - **Supported filters:** `Name`, `SystemId`, `ExternalId`, `Id`
  - **Supported filter operators :** `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `ge`,`lt`, `le`, `like`, `in`, `nin`.
  
- **`$search`** (search by Name or Part Number): (e.g., /api/product/v1/Products?$search='Product1')
            
- **`$orderby`** (Sorting results):
  - Sorts results in ascending (`asc`) order by default.
  - **Supported fields:** `Name`, `ExternalId`, `Id`.
  
### Example:
- {{url}}/api/product/v1/Products?$filter=name eq 'Oil' and systemId eq 'Oil_cpq'&$expand=Attributes,localScripts&$top=2;

post.summary: Inserts a new product or creates a new version of an existing product.
post.description:
This API allows you to either create a new product or add a new version of an existing product.

### Creating or Updating a Product:
- If the provided `System_ID` **already exists**, a **new version** of the product will be created.
- The new version will be **active** if `isActiveVersion` is set to `true` in the request.
- `BasicInfo` is **mandatory** and must be provided.

### Auto-Creation of Related Entities:
- If the `productType` **does not exist**, it will be **automatically created**.
- The same applies to **categories** and **shipping** information:
  - If they exist, they will be linked to the new product.
  - If they **don’t exist**, they will be **created**.

### Handling Product Attributes:
- If `attributes`, `advancedRules`, `simpleRules`, `productValidationMessages`, `localScripts`, 
  `globalScripts`, `aliasAttributesInfo`, `layout`, `codeMatching`, or `translations` are **null**, 
  only the product itself will be created.
- To add product attributes:
  - Use `attributes:{}` to define new attributes.
  - If an attribute **already exists**, just reference it (no need to redefine).
  - If adding a **new** attribute, include both:
    1. **Attribute Definition** (what the attribute is)
    2. **Product Attribute Info** (how the product uses it)
- The same logic applies to `productAttributeValues`.

### Handling Connected Objects:
- To add **connected objects**, populate:
  - `advancedRules`
  - `simpleRules`
  - `productValidationMessages`
  in case of any product that ConfigurationType is different than Variant
  in case of ConfigurationType is Variant
  - `visibilityRestrictions`
  for any kind of product:
  - `localScripts`
  - `globalScripts`
  - `layout`
  - `codeMatching`
  - `translations`
- **Alias Attributes:**  
  - If creating an alias, set `aliasParentSystemId` (System_ID of the original product).
  - Populate `aliasAttributesInfo` (other objects are ignored for aliases).

### Category Handling:
- If a **Category** with the given `System_ID` **does not exist**, it will be created.
- If a `parentSystemId` is provided and the parent category **does not exist**, it will also be created.
- Categories added via this API always have a **rank of 100**.

### Permissions and Visibility:
- You can only assign **existing permissions** to a product (new permissions are **not created**).
- If `visibilityToEveryone = true`, permissions will be **ignored**.

### Shipping:
- If **no shipping information** is provided, it remains unchanged.
- To **define new shipping**, add it to the request.
- To **change an existing shipping expression**, specify the shipping and set the expression.
- To set an **empty expression**, include the shipping and set the expression to an empty string.

### SalesUnitsOfMeasure:
- Applicable only for products synced from back office.
- If **no sales unit of measure** is provided, it remains unchanged.
- To **define sales unit of measure**, specify the unit add it to the request.

get.summary: Retrieves a product by its ID.
get.description:
### Performance Considerations:
- If the system uses **many dictionaries**, consider **omitting translations** to improve performance.
            
### Supported Query Parameters:
- **`$expand`** (Include additional details in response):
  - By default, only **root-level** product information is returned.
  - Supported values:
    - `All` → Returns **all details**.
    - `Attributes`, `GlobalScripts`, `LocalScripts`, `AliasAttributesInfo`, `AdvancedRules`,
      `SimpleRules`, `ProductValidationMessages`, `Layout`, `CodeMatching`, `Translations`, `SalesUnitsOfMeasure`, `VisibilityRestrictions`.
  - **Example:**  
    - `$expand=All` → Returns all details.  
    - `$expand=Attributes` → Returns **only Attributes** + root-level information.
    
### Example:
- {{url}}/api/product/v1/Products/38?$expand=Attributes,LocalScripts

put.summary: Updates an existing product with the provided ID.
put.description:
### Update Behavior:
- **Full replacement**: This operation **fully updates** the product, replacing all existing data.
- **BasicInfo is mandatory**: The product **must** include `BasicInfo` in the update request.
- **Dependent objects**: If modified, the following objects will also be updated:
  - `AdvancedRules`, `SimpleRules`, `ProductValidationMessages`,
  in case of any product that ConfigurationType is different than Variant
  in case of ConfigurationType is Variant
  - `VisibilityRestrictions`
  for any kind of product
    `Attributes`, `LocalScripts`, `GlobalScripts`, `SubscriptionBilling`, `AliasAttributesInfo`,
    `Layout`, `CodeMatching`, `Translations`.
            
### Product Type and Category Handling:
- **Product Type**:
  - If the `ProductType` does **not** exist, it will be **created** and assigned to the product.
- **Category**:
  - If the category with the provided `SystemID` does **not** exist, it will be **created**.
  - If `parentSystemId` is provided and does **not** exist, it will also be created and set as the parent.
  - Multiple categories can be assigned, and new categories created via this API will always have `rank = 100`.
            
### Permissions and Visibility:
- **Permissions**:
  - Only **existing permissions** can be assigned to a product.
  - **New permissions cannot be created automatically**.
- **Visibility**:
  - If `visibilityToEveryone = true`, any defined permissions will be **ignored**.
            
### Shipping and Code Matching:
- **Shipping**:
  - If no shipping is provided, **no changes** will be made.
  - If shipping is included in the payload:
    - A **new shipping** definition can be created and automatically assigned to the product.
    - To **remove shipping expression**, explicitly set it to an **empty string**.
- **Code Matching**:
  - Only **new code matches** will be inserted.
  - Any **code matches not provided** in the update request will be **removed**.

parameters.name: productIntegration
parameters.description: The product data for the update.

parameters.description: The ID of the product in the destination system.

patch.summary: Partially updates a product or any of its sub-objects.
patch.description:
### Patch Behavior:
- **Basic Info is Required**:  
  - The `id` of the product **must** be included in the request.
  - Any other fields that need updating within `BasicInfo` must also be included.
            
- **Updating Sub-Objects**:  
  - To update a specific sub-object (e.g., `LocalScripts`, `AdvancedScripts`), include its section in the payload.
  - Each sub-object **must** have a unique `SystemId`, which the system uses to match and update the correct object.
  - Only the provided properties will be updated; others will remain unchanged.
            
### Example Payload:
```json
{
  "basicInfo": {
    "id": 6296
  },
  "localScripts": [
    {
      "systemId": "Local_script_cpq",
      "description": "Updated Description"
    }
  ]
}
```
**Explanation**:
- This request updates **product ID 6296**.
- It modifies **the `LocalScripts` sub-object** with `systemId: "Local_script_cpq"`.
- Only the `description` field is updated, while other properties remain unchanged.

parameters.name: productIntegration
parameters.description: The product data containing the fields to update.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the product to be updated.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of bulk operation statuses based on query conditions.
get.description:
### Supported Query Parameters:
            
- **Pagination:**  
  - **`$top`** → Number of records to return (default: **10**, max: **1000**).  
  - **`$skip`** → Number of records to skip.
            
- **Filtering (`$filter`)** → Supports filtering using the following fields:  
  - `Operation`, `Status`, `Message`, `ProductSystemId`, `ProductId`, `Id`.
  - Multiple conditions can be combined using **AND**.
            
- **Sorting (`$orderby`)** → Defines sorting order (default: **ascending**).  
  - Supports sorting by: `Operation`, `Status`, `Message`, `ProductSystemId`, `ProductId`, `Id`.

get.summary: Get all quote custom fields.
get.description:
This endpoint is available for both Quote 1 and Quote 2.
If using Quote 1 then CalulationType field is used for indicating event which triggers field recalculation, for Quote 2 CalculationEvents field should be used.
You can use the following request to retrieve quote custom fields that match the specified filters. This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Filtering Guidelines: - For the showOnPlaceOrder filter, only numeric values are allowed (e.g., '0' or '1'). Do not use 'true' or 'false'.
Example Requests: /api/quote/v1/quoteCustomField?$filter=name eq 'example' /api/quote/v1/quoteCustomField?$filter=showInTab like '5' /api/quote/v1/quoteCustomField?$filter=type eq 'FreeForm' /api/quote/v1/quoteCustomField?$filter=showOnPlaceOrder eq '0' /api/quote/v1/quoteCustomField?$filter=showInTabNames eq 'Quotation' /api/quote/v1/quoteCustomField?$filter=isProtected eq 'true' The following attribute properties are available to OData, and they have a specific support for the $filter option.
name - $filter eq, ne, like label - $filter eq, ne, like showInTab - $filter like type - $filter eq, ne showInTabNames - $filter eq, ne, like isProtected - $filter eq, ne tabRank - $filter eq, ne showOnPlaceOrder - $filter eq, ne Additional OData options are available for these properties: $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} (Ordering by 'label' is not supported) $top={number, max 100} $skip={number} $expand=usertypelabels $expand=translations $search="custom field name"

post.description:
To add items to a quote, the endpoint allows a maximum limit of 100 items. If you need to add more than 100 items, you should make an additional API call using the following endpoint: /api/v1/quotes/{quoteId}/items.
If MarketId is not provided, the system will resolve MarketId based on provided MarketCode and CurrencyCode, if none are provided, the default market will be used for the logged in user. CustomerId takes priority over CustomerCode so if both are provided, the system disregards the CustomerCode and looks for the customer based on the CustomerId. Customer RoleType accepts 1 for billto customer, 2 for shipto customer, and 3 for enduser.
PricebookId takes priority over DistributionChannel so if both are provided, the system disregards the DistributionChannel and looks for the pricebook based on the PricebookId. SAP CPQ looks for the pricebook with the respective DistributionChannel in the market provided in MarketId.

post.summary: Executes the action (either standard or custom, both are supported).
post.description:
The following actions don’t need additional parameters: New Active Revision (actionId: 64) , Set Active (actionId: 15) , Accept Proposal (actionId: 41) , Reject Proposal (actionId: 42) , Copy (actionId: 4), Delete (actionId: 2), Place Order (actionId: 10), Create/Update Objects From Quote Items (actionId: 2517), Create Assets (actionId: 54), Create Update SAP Sales Quote (actionId: 1811), Create/Update Opportunity (actionId: 2500), Create Custom Object From Quote (actionId: 2519), CreateQuoteInCRM (actionId: 2523), Detach From Opportunity (actionId: 36), Upgrade to New Product Version (actionId: 51), Post Quote Notes Into Account Chatter (actionId: 57), Post Quote Notes Into Opportunity Chatter (actionId: 55), Send Document to CRM (actionId: 1655), Update SAP Opportunity (actionId: 1810), Attach Document To SAP Opportunity (actionId: 1820), Attach Document To SAP Sales Quote (actionId: 1821), Attach Document To SAP Opportunity And Sales Quote (actionId: 1822), Place order to ERP (actionId: 1823), Update Sales Quote (actionId: 1825), Make Primary (actionId: 73) and Release Quote To SAP Commerce (actionId: 1826).

The following actions require additional parameters in the request body:
- New Active Revision (actionId: 64) should contain the NewRevisionName parameter. This parameter is optional, and it contains the name of the newly created revision.
- Reassign (actionId: 6) must contain the NewUserId parameter. This parameter is mandatory, and it contains the ID of the user to whom the quote should be assigned.
- Change Status (actionId: 12) must contain the NewStatusId parameter. This parameter is mandatory, and it contains the ID of the new status of the quote.
- Integration Change Quote Status (actionId: 1922) must contain the NewStatusId parameter. This parameter is mandatory, and it contains the ID of the new status of the quote. NewStatusId must correspond with the Workflow settings. Available statuses are Order Placed, Order Created With Errors and Creating Order Failed. The Integration Change Quote Status can also contain the following optional parameters: SalesOrderID, which contains the ID of the sales order, and RemovePreviousMessages, which is a boolean (default value is false) used to remove existing error messages from the quote. The ErrorMessage that is logged after Creating Order Failed or Order Created With Errors is triggered from SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management. The message describes the issue that caused the quote synchronization to fail.
- Order Status Update (actionId: 1923) must contain the SalesOrderID parameter. This parameter is mandatory, and it contains the ID of the sales order.
- Creating Order Failed (actionId: 1924) must contains these parameters: SalesOrderID parameter is mandatory, and it contains the ID of the sales order. ErrorMessage that is being logged after Creating Order Failed is triggered from Billing Revenue and Innovation Management.The message describes the issue that caused the quote synchronization to fail.
- Release Quote And Proposal To SAP Commerce (actionId: 1824) must contain the ProposalMessage parameter. This parameter is required, and it contains the message that will be sent with the quote and the proposal.
- Submit for Approval (actionId: 25) must contain the list of approval rules. Each rule must contain these parameters: Rule contains the rule ID, Approvers contains the list of approvers (user IDs) and Comment contains the rule comment. Sample Payload : { "Approvals": [ { "Rule": approval rule ID, "Approvers":[userId's], "Comment": "any comment" } ] }
- Approve or Reject Quote (Approve Quote Action Id: 26, Reject Quote Action Id: 27) must contain the list of ApproversResponsibilities. Each responsibility must contain these parameters: ResponsibilityId contains the Responsibility ID, Comment contains the Responsibility comment.
- Get Status of Placed Order (actionId: 1926) must contain these parameters: SalesOrderID parameter is mandatory, and it contains the ID of the sales order.
- Retract approval (actionId: 53) must contain the Comment parameter. Payload example : { "Comment" : "some comment" }

200.description: New revision and New active revision actions return the NewQuoteId and other actions return an empty response.

get.description: Gets the details of all involved parties on the quote (for example, partner function ID and key, partner function name, business partner ID, and so on).

403.description: The user doesn't have the rights to perform the action or the Business Partners feature is not enabled.

post.summary: Adds the involved party to the specified quote.
post.description:
If the partner function with the specified key exists in the Setup, the system searches for the business partner with the business partner ID specified in the body. If there is a business partner with that ID in the Setup, the system uses it to create the involved party. The details of the business partner defined in the Setup are overwritten on the quote with the details sent in the body. If there aren't any business partners with that ID in the Setup, an adequate response is sent.

If the body of the request doesn’t contain the business partner ID, the system uses the partner ID to find the business partner. If there is a business partner with the matching partner ID in the Setup, the system uses it to create the involved party. The details of the business partner defined in the Setup are overwritten on the quote with the details sent in the body.

If there is a matching business partner ID in the Setup, but the partner ID from the body doesn’t match any of the partner IDs in the Setup, the system overwrites the partner ID of the business partner with the ID from the body.

If the body of the request doesn’t contain the business partner ID nor the partner ID, the system uses the external ID to find the business partner. If there is a business partner with the matching external ID in the Setup, the system uses it to create the involved party. The details of the business partner defined in the Setup are overwritten on the quote with the details sent in the body.

If there is a matching business partner ID and partner ID in the Setup, but the external ID from the body doesn’t match any of the external IDs in the Setup, the system overwrites the external ID of the business partner with the ID from the body.

If the request body doesn’t contain the business partner ID nor the partner ID nor the external ID, the system creates a new business partner on the quote with the details sent in the body. This scenario is applicable only if the Allow adding business partners on quotes without creating them first in Setup parameter is enabled in the tenant.

403.description: The user doesn't have the rights to perform the action or the Business Partners feature is not enabled.

404.description: Requested partner function does not exist.

delete.description: Removes the involved party from the quote, while the business partner and the partner function remain unchanged in the Setup.

Updates the partner function and business partner details on the quote while they stay unchanged in the Setup. You can't create new business partners through this API, you can only update the existing ones.
Names of custom fields must be prefixed with 'cf_' like  'cf_CustomFieldName'

get.summary: Retrieve a list of groups
get.description:
This endpoint enables retrieval of a collection of groups. The first 100 group members are retrieved in one call by default.
Example: /api/scim/v2/Groups?filter=id eq "Administrator"&startIndex=5&count=30 Filtering using the "eg" operator is supported for the following columns:
id, displayName In case of multiple conditions, the AND logic is applied.

parameters.name: pagingParameterModel.count
parameters.description: Maximum size of the returned collection.

get.summary: Retrieve details of a group
get.description: The first 100 group members are retrieved in one call by default. To get a full list of group members (for example users), use GET user collection with a filter.

patch.summary: Unassign or assign users to a group
patch.description:
<para>We support matching with "members" and "members[value eq "{userid}"]" paths.</para>
<para>Other paths are invalid.</para>
<para>For path "members", we only support "add" and "replace" operations.</para>
<para>For path "members[value eq "{userid}"]", we only support the "remove" operation.</para>
<para>For groups that represent a user type in SAP CPQ (suffixed with -USERTYPE), removing a group member will have no effect, while adding a group member will simultaneously remove the member from the original user type group.</para>

get.summary: Retrieve details of a user

put.description: The groups will also be re-assigned to the user or unassigned from them.

409.description: Username conflict.

400.description: The logged user cannot be deleted.

patch.summary: Activate or deactivate a user or change user groups
patch.description:
<para>We support matching with "active", "groups" and "groups[value eq "{groupid}"]" paths.</para>
<para>Other paths are invalid.</para>
<para>For the "active" path, we support "add", "replace" and "remove" operations.</para>
<para>For the "groups" path, we support "add" and "replace" operations.</para>
<para>The "add" operation adds the provided groups (if the user already belongs to the provided group, that group is ignored).</para>
<para>The "replace" operation removes the user from all the groups they belonged to, and then assigns them to the newly provided groups (a group with the -USERTYPE suffix needs to be provided, and it needs to be a system ID of a valid SAP CPQ user type).</para>
<para>The "remove" operation removes a group (the group with -USERTYPE suffix will be ignored for the "remove" operation, as it is a required field).</para>

get.summary: Retrieve a list of users
get.description:
Retrieves a collection of users.
Example: /api/scim/v2/Users?startindex=3&count=15&filter=groups eq "admin" API supports filtering the following columns using the "eg" operator:
id, userName, groups, emails, name.familyName and:
meta.lastModified for operators "eq", "gt", "ge", "lt" and "le". The filter supports AND logic between conditions.

parameters.name: pagingParameterModel.count
parameters.description: Maximum size of the returned collection.

get.summary:
Get all usage based pricing tables related to quote items.
For each table, we will get its name, description, tableType as well as its cross catalog mapping data.

Primarily intended to be used with a filter, such as ?$filter=quoteId eq '1' in order to retrieve all usage based tables on one specific quote.
If we want all tables related to an item, we could use ?$filter=quoteItemId eq '1'.

Gets usage based pricing table content with specified table system Id.
In case of range tables, the data will come in form of rows and ranges, both containing input and output columns respectively.
If we are getting mapping table content, then all data will be returned in the row (both input and output columns).

There is also a possibility to expand table definition (expand=definition) as well as table columns (expand=columns).
Gets usage based pricing table row.
Export table to a file.

post.summary:
Upload the usage based table and associate it with appropriate quote item.
Route can be used to upload mapping and range pricing tables.
Route can upload files in the .xlsx format.
There is limitation on the file size which is 20MB.
File should contain only one sheet, which will be used for import.
Insert redefined usages based table content for a table system id and update table sync status.
post.description:
This method updates the table content for the specified table system id by synchronizing the
            provided data or update the table sync status in case of error message. 
            Ensure that the {quoteItemId} corresponds to a valid quote item and that
            the  {redefineTableContentForAsset} contains all required fields.

parameters.name: quoteItemId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the quote item for which the table content is being synchronized. Must be a
            positive integer.

parameters.name: redefineTableContentForAsset
parameters.description:
An object containing the table content. This parameter cannot be null.
            
            TableContent - Table rows content in CSV format. to insert data to the provided redefine table system id.
            ErrorMessage - Error message if the operation failed. This will set sync status to fail for the provided redefine table system id.

post.summary: Resynchronizes the asset table for the specified quote item and default table system IDs.
post.description:
This operation is used to resynchronize asset tables for a specific quote item.
            It requires valid authentication and authorization.

parameters.name: quoteItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the quote item for which the asset table should be resynchronized.

parameters.name: defaultTableSystemIds
parameters.description:
A list of system IDs representing the default tables to be resynchronized. This parameter cannot be null or
            empty.

post.summary: Insert cross catalog mapping verions related to its products, along with their charge plans and tables.
post.description:
The structure we obtain should look like this:
1. Product
    1.1 Cross Catalog Mapping
        1.1.1 Charge Plan
            1.1.1.1. Table
After this sync, we should know which tables belong to what products.

parameters.name: productCatalogMappingVM
parameters.description: Meta data for the product and its cross catalog mappings.

get.summary: Gets the filtered, paged and sorted list of users with their total number
get.description:
This endpoint supports custom filtering and pagination.
Example:
setup/api/v1/admin/users?username_like=james&offset=10&limit=10&sort=-firstName
The result can be filtered with the following properties using only the listed operators:
username_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like firstName_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like lastName_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like email_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like userType_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like company_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like address_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like approvalParent_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like managingParent_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like orderingParent_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like territory_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like active_{operator} - operator: eq,ne createdBy_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like dateCreated_{operator} - operator: eq,lt,gt,ge,le modifiedBy_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like dateModified_{operator} - operator: eq,lt,gt,ge,le federationId_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like zipCode_{operator} - operator: ne,eq,like There are additional options available for request: sort={-/+}{property name} limit={number} offset={number}

get.summary: Gets the list of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing Event Logs.
get.description:
This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Example: /api/eventLog/v1/vcIntegrationEventLogs?$filter=label eq 'example' /api/eventLog/v1/vcIntegrationEventLogs?$filter=label ne 'example' /api/eventLog/v1/vcIntegrationEventLogs?$filter=label like 'example' /api/eventLog/v1/vcIntegrationEventLogs?$filter=label eq 'example' and domain ne 'default' and url like 'url' /api/eventLog/v1/vcIntegrationEventLogs?$filter=creationTime gt '2021-8-12' /api/eventLog/v1/vcIntegrationEventLogs?$filter=creationTime lt '2021-8-12' /api/eventLog/v1/vcIntegrationEventLogs?$select=label,creationTime The following properties of the log entries for the SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing integration are available via OData, and specifically support the $filter option.
label - $filter eq,ne,like creationTime - $filter eq,ne,like,gt,lt userName - $filter eq,ne,like, sessionId - $filter eq,ne,like, message - $filter eq,ne,like domain - $filter eq,ne,like ticks - $filter eq,ne,like kbNumber - $filter eq,ne,like kbSyncGuid - $filter eq,ne,like url - $filter eq,ne,like Furthermore, an additional OData option is available for the properties above:
$orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number, max 100} $skip={number} $select={label} For more information, please refer to the OData syntax documentation: https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/

get.summary: Get workflow permissions.
get.description:
This endpoint supports OData filtering.
Example: api/workflow/v1/permissions?$filter=actionId eq '1' api/workflow/v1/permissions?$filter=userTypeId eq '10' The following attribute properties are available to OData, and have specific support for $filter option.
actionId - $filter eq,lt,gt,ge,le userTypeId - $filter eq,lt,gt,ge,le Additional OData option are available for these properties: $orderby={properties name} {desc/asc} $top={number, max 100} $skip={number} For more information, check the OData syntax documentation https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/

200.description: List of user types assigned to each action according to workflow permissions.

post.summary: Replace the user types assigned to an action with a new set of user types.
post.description:
Request data in body is formatted in the same way it is return from GET,
but it is important to know that only IDs are taken into consideration.
This means that User Type Name and Action Name cannot be updated through this method call. This endpoint will replace the existing user types with new ones within body request,
only if the action and the user types already exist in the system.
If validation for one permission fails, all permissions are rejected.

200.description: Updated user types for specific actions.

404.description: Action or user type not found.

delete.summary: Deletes the workflow permissions that are assigned to a user type for an action.
delete.description: User Type and Action cannot be deleted through this method call, only workflow permissions existing user type and action is deleted if exists.

204.description: The user type with the provided ID is removed from the workflow permissions for the specified action.

400.description: The workflow permissions for the user type can't be deleted for the specified action.

404.description: Action or user type not found.

500.description: Error deleting the user type from the workflow permissions for the specified action.

Success.description: Indicates if reassigning quote was success

RedirectToURL.description: Next URL where user should be redirected to

Id.description: Identifier of the pricing condition record.

Base.description: Condition base. A scalar multiplier used to calculate the condition value from the condition rate.

CalculationType.description: This field specifies how the pricing engine calculates the condition value. The pricing engine can calculate a condition value in different ways, for example in its absolute amount, as a percentage, etc.

ChangeOfCalculationTypeAllowed.description: The indicator that controls if a change of calculation type is allowed.

ChangeOfRateAllowed.description: The indicator that controls if a change of condition amount/percentage is allowed.

ChangeOfValueAllowed.description: The indicator that controls if a change of condition value is allowed.

ChangedManually.description: The indicator that shows whether the condition was manually changed.

ConditionTypeId.description: Name of a condition type. In pricing, the pricing elements are depicted using condition types. Pricing elements can be prices, surcharges, or discounts. Returns 'null' if the condition step represents a subtotal.

ConditionTypeName.description: Language-dependent description of the condition type as defined in customizing. In case of variant conditions, the description of the variant key will be returned. In case of external conditions, the description passed externally will be returned. If a description is maintained at the condition record level, it can be returned based on an engine parameter setting. Note: This is not applicable for variant conditions. The description for accumulated conditions at the document level always reflects the one maintained in the condition type customizing.

Control.description: This field records manual changes and controls how a condition is handled. It helps to reconstruct the history of the condition in a document.

Counter.description: The current number of conditions in a step in a pricing procedure.

DeletionAllowed.description: An indicator that controls if deletion is allowed.

DurationFactor.description: The duration factor corresponds to the condition's calculation type: M-months, N-years, O-days, P-weeks. Duration factor is applied to the product of condition rate and quantity to calculate the condition value for the given contract duration.

ExchangeRate.description: The exchange rate used for conversion of condition rate/value from condition currency to local currency.

InactiveFlag.description: Status of the condition. For example: condition status will be made inactive if there is an error during currency conversion

IsVariantCondition.description: Indicates whether the condition is a variant condition.

PricingDate.description: The date used for the price calculation of a condition.

Purpose.description: The condition function/purpose field displays subtotals for conditions with similar business purposes. Used by SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP CPQ to distinguish and display the base price and surcharges during the product configuration.

Rate.description: Amount or percentage rate for a condition types. For example: If the determined condition record is 30.00 EUR per 2 PCE, then this field holds the value as 30.00 and unit as EUR.

RateConverted.description:
The field holds the condition rate information converted to the document currency.
If the condition currency is P1 or if the condition currency and the document currency are the same, the field holds the same value as in 'rate' field.
For example: If the determined condition record is 30.00 EUR per 2 PCE, and if the document currency is USD, then this field holds the value in USD per 2 PCE.
In case the 'value' of the condition is manually updated, this field will hold the condition rate in the document currency unit or P1 derived from condition value depending upon the calculation type.
Note: While creating quotation/order in the backend via order API's unit and value belonging to 'rate' field should be mapped. This field should not be used. If any error occurs during currency conversion, this field will be set to null.

RateNormalized.description: This field is intended for the integration of the Pricing service with SAP CPQ and can go through breaking changes based on new CPQ requirements without further notice. Do not use this field in other integrations!

RateQuantity.description: Condition Unit Value. For example: If the determined condition record is 30.00 EUR per 2 PCE, then this field holds the value 2 and unit as PCE.

RoundingDifference.description: The pricing engine compares the condition value at the header level with the total of the condition values at the item level for group conditions. This rounding difference is added to the largest item.

Statistical.description: Statistical conditions are not considered in the net price calculations.

StepNumber.description: The number that specifies the sequence of a condition in the pricing procedure.

SubProcedureId.description: Name of the sub-procedure determined for the inclusion point. If no sub procedure is determined it is set to "" at item level. At header level it is set to "" if different sub procedures or no sub procedures are determined for the inclusion point at item level.

Value.description: The value resulting from pricing, for a condition, total, or subtotal in a document. The value is expressed in the document currency.

VariantKeyFactor.description: Technical identifier for the variant condition. It consists of the variant key and the factor that is a multiplier originating from Variant Configuration service that applies to the pricing condition.

SAP.CPQ.API.Configurator.VM.SalesAreaVM.description: Sales area is a combination of Sales Organization, Distribution Channel and Division.

SalesOrganization.description:
The organizational unit that sells and distributes products, negotiates terms of sale,
and is responsible for these transactions, as well as legal obligations.

DistributionChannel.description:
A channel through which an organisation makes its products/services reach the customers.
e.g. Direct Sales, Wholesale, Retail etc.

Division.description: Division is a way of categorising or grouping products or services.

intervalType.description:
Interval type of the characteristic to distinguish different types of domain elements. 
String/Symbolic characteristic values have either type 0 or 1. 
If it is numeric, ‘1’: single value x == lower boundary, ‘2’: [a,b) (right) half open interval, 
‘3’: [a,b] closed interval, ‘4’: (a,b) open interval, 
‘5’: (a,b] (left) half open interval, ‘6’: (-infinity, b), 
‘7’: (-infinity, b], ‘8’: (a, +infinity), 
‘9’: [a, +infinity), ‘0’: unconstrained domain
Enum: [ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 0 ]

Webcom.API.Scim.Models.ScimV2ResourceQue.description: defines the response on querying resources

resources.description: the list of resources (Users or Groups)

id.description:
ID of the group.
In SAP CPQ, it is the system ID of user types with "-USERTYPE" (case-insensitive) suffix and custom permission groups and 'ADMINISTRATOR' (case-insensitive) for CPQ admin user.

displayName.description: The displayed name. In CPQ it represents the name of the custom permission group, 'CPQ Administrator' for admin and the user type name of the user type.

members.description: the user refenceses of those in this group

created.description: the time when the resource is created in UTC datetime format. For example: 2011-08-01T18:29:49Z

lastModified.description: the time when the resource is last modified in UTC datetime format. For example: 2011-08-01T18:29:49Z

location.description: the relative string representation of the uri which points to the resource

Webcom.API.Scim.Models.ScimV2Error.description: defines a scim error reponse payload. See https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644#section-3.12

Webcom.API.Scim.Models.ScimV2PatchGroupR.description: represents group patching request for assigning group members

path.description:
paths allow 'members' and 'members[value eq "{{userId}}"]'. 
For path 'members' operations 'add' and 'replace' are allowed. 
For path 'members[value eq "{{userId}}"]' only operation 'remove' is allowed.

value.description: Data to be processed corresponds to the operation. It's only for adding members or replacing all members of a group.

Webcom.API.Scim.Models.ScimV2JsonPatchOp.description: Represents a piece of data to be processed during assigning members of a group

Webcom.API.Scim.Models.ScimV2PatchGroupR.description: represents group patching request for assigning group members
represents group patching request for assigning group members

id.description: The identifier of the user which in CPQ.

userName.description: login user name of the user which in CPQ is the user name. It is case-insensitive, unique and cannot be 'system'.

urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:e.description: the company to which the user belongs to

emails.description: emails of the user. In CPQ we manage only one email

phoneNumbers.description: phone of the user. In CPQ we manage only the mobile and fax

addresses.description: the address of the user. In CPQ we only manage one 'home' address

groups.description: the group references to which the user belongs to

Webcom.API.Scim.Models.ScimV2Name.description: defines the name of the user

organization.description: the company system ID in CPQ and it is case-insensitive

primary.description: true if the email is the primary one. If multiple emails are given, only the primary email will be managed in CPQ.

type.description: the phone number type. If multiple phone numbers are given, only the types of 'work' and 'fax' will be mapped.
type of the address. If more addresses are given, only the type of 'home' will be mapped.

value.description:
ID of the group. 
In CPQ it is the system ID of user types with "-USERTYPE" suffix and custom permission groups and 'ADMINISTRATOR' for CPQ admin user

$ref.description: the relative URL of the group

isSsoUser.description: Determine if the user is SSO user

customFields.description: Collection of CPQ User Custom fields.

lastLoginDate.description: Represents date and time of last user's login. It is UTC time in ISO format.

Webcom.API.Scim.Models.ScimV2PatchOperat.description: The patching schema here is dedicated for changing active status or user groups.

path.description:
Allowed paths are 'active', 'groups' and 'groups[value eq "{{groupId}}"]'.
For path 'active' operations 'add', 'remove' and 'replace' are allowed.
For path 'groups' operations 'add' and 'replace' are allowed.
For path 'groups[value eq "{{groupId}}"]' only operation 'remove' is allowed.

value.description:
Processed data corresponds to the operation in use.
In case of the 'groups' path, only operations 'add' and 'replace' can be used for all groups.
Groups are represented as a collection of {Webcom.API.Scim.Models.ScimV2GroupReference} objects.
In case of the 'active' path, only the active value can be changed by operations 'add' or 'replace'.
Active value could be true or false.

Represents the Quote List configuration model that contains both
Visibility Rules (columns) and Quote List Filters for a specific user type.

userTypeId.description: Gets or sets the identifier of the user type for which the configuration applies.

columns.description: Gets or sets the list of columns (Visibility Rules) included in this configuration.

filters.description: Gets or sets the list of filters associated with this configuration.

quoteTypeName.description: The QuoteType (TabName) value e.g. 1 - My Quotes, 2 - Waiting For Approval, etc.

quoteTypeId.description: The QuoteType value e.g. 1 - My Quotes, 2 - Waiting For Approval, etc.
